title: ophrys oxyrrhynchos subsp. calliantha
intro: Ophrys oxyrrhynchos subsp. calliantha, also known as the orchid bee orchid, is a subspecies of the Ophrys oxyrrhynchos plant, a type of orchid native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Ophrys oxyrrhynchos subsp. calliantha include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and soothe digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, decoctions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human use.
